proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months — i.e., PFS decreases as ecog_ps increases from 0 to 2.
Patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mcrpc=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without (mcrpc=0).
Patients with visceral metastases (visceral_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: PFS falls monotonically across ECOG strata: ECOG 0 mean 4.68 (n=17592), ECOG 1 mean 3.49 (n=24971), ECOG 2 mean 2.38 (n=7437). OLS slope -1.16 months per unit increase in ECOG.
mCRPC patients have mean PFS 3.51 vs 4.03 in non-mCRPC (difference -0.52 months).
Visceral mets patients have mean PFS 3.71 vs 3.75 in those without — a small negative difference.
Contrary to the directional prediction, age has a strongly POSITIVE association with PFS: slope +0.174 months per year of age. Direction opposite to hypothesis but extremely significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater weight loss in 6 months (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher PSA (psa_ng_ml, including its log transform) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Lower hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline phosphatase (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher Gleason score (gleason_score) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Albumin is strongly positively associated with PFS, slope +0.495 months per g/dL.
LDH is negatively associated with PFS, slope -0.00042 per U/L (small per-unit effect, large at population scale).
Greater 6-mo weight loss is strongly negatively associated with PFS, -0.075 months per percentage point lost.
log(1+PSA) is strongly negatively associated with PFS, slope -0.382. Untransformed PSA also negative (-0.0033 per ng/mL).
CRP not associated with PFS in unadjusted analysis (slope -0.00064).
NLR not associated with PFS (slope +0.0024, opposite sign to expected).
Hemoglobin shows a small NEGATIVE univariate slope (-0.012), opposite to the prediction. Marginally significant; attenuates fully in multivariable adjustment (later iteration).
Alkaline phosphatase is weakly negatively associated with PFS, slope -0.0005 per U/L.
Gleason score is not associated with PFS (slope -0.006). Mean PFS is essentially flat across Gleason 6 through 10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with enzalutamide (treatment_enzalutamide=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than without.
Treatment with abiraterone (treatment_abiraterone=1) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Treatment with docetaxel (treatment_docetaxel=1) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Treatment with olaparib (treatment_olaparib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months in the unselected cohort.
Treatment with Lu-177-PSMA (treatment_lu177_psma=1) is associated with longer pfs_months in the unselected cohort.
Treatment with pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months in the unselected cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide on vs off: 3.728 vs 3.755 months (diff -0.026). Not significant.
Abiraterone on vs off: 3.740 vs 3.746 months (diff -0.006). Not significant.
Docetaxel on vs off: 3.732 vs 3.749 (diff -0.017). Not significant.
Olaparib on vs off: 3.829 vs 3.735 (diff +0.094). Significant in unadjusted analysis but driven by BRCA2-mutated subgroup (see iter 4).
Lu-177-PSMA on vs off: 3.741 vs 3.745 (diff -0.004). Not significant.
Pembrolizumab on vs off: 3.805 vs 3.741 (diff +0.064). Not significant overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between brca2_mutation and treatment_olaparib on pfs_months: olaparib's PFS benefit is larger in BRCA2-mutated patients than in BRCA2 wild-type, consistent with PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality.

analyses.result_summary: Massive positive interaction. Stratified means: BRCA2-/olaparib- 3.74 (n=40435); BRCA2-/olaparib+ 3.67 (n=4569); BRCA2+/olaparib- 3.67 (n=4467); BRCA2+/olaparib+ 5.22 (n=529). Interaction coefficient +1.62 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between msi_high and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: pembrolizumab benefit is larger in MSI-high patients (the on-label predictive biomarker).

analyses.result_summary: No significant interaction. Stratified means: MSI-/pembro- 3.74; MSI-/pembro+ 3.81; MSI+/pembro- 3.79; MSI+/pembro+ 3.53 (only 79 patients). Interaction coefficient -0.34 (opposite to expectation), but p-value not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between psma_high and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months: Lu-177-PSMA benefit is larger in PSMA-high patients.

analyses.result_summary: No interaction. Stratified means: PSMA-/Lu- 3.73; PSMA-/Lu+ 3.74; PSMA+/Lu- 3.75; PSMA+/Lu+ 3.74. Interaction coefficient -0.017.

proposed_hypotheses.text: AR-V7 positivity (ar_v7_positive=1) interacts negatively with treatment_enzalutamide: enzalutamide is less effective (or harmful) in AR-V7 positive patients (resistance biomarker).
AR-V7 positivity interacts negatively with treatment_abiraterone for the same reason.
AR-V7 positivity has a negative main effect on pfs_months (worse prognosis).

analyses.result_summary: No interaction (coef +0.0025, p=0.96). Hypothesized resistance pattern not detected.
No interaction (coef +0.048, p=0.33). Hypothesized resistance pattern not detected.
AR-V7+ vs AR-V7- mean PFS 3.734 vs 3.747 (diff -0.013). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bone metastases (bone_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Liver metastases (liver_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Adrenal metastases (adrenal_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Pleural effusion (pleural_effusion=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Pericardial effusion (pericardial_effusion=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Contralateral lung metastases (contralateral_lung_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: bone_mets: 3.753 vs 3.742 (+0.011). Not significant; direction opposite to prediction.
liver_mets: 3.743 vs 3.744 (-0.002). Not significant.
adrenal_mets: 3.782 vs 3.740 (+0.042). Not significant; direction opposite.
pleural_effusion: 3.707 vs 3.751 (-0.043). Not significant (p=0.088).
pericardial_effusion: 3.710 vs 3.746 (-0.036). Not significant.
contralateral_lung_mets: 3.742 vs 3.744 (-0.002). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history, autoimmune_disease, hepatitis_b/c, hiv_positive, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis, interstitial_lung_disease_history) are individually associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: None of 15 comorbidity flags shows a significant association with PFS at alpha=0.05. The smallest p was depression_anxiety_diagnosis (-0.045 months, p=0.059). Magnitudes uniformly small (|diff|<0.07 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cough_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: fatigue_grade slope +0.0036; not significant; direction opposite expected.
pain_nrs slope -0.0009; not significant.
dyspnea_grade slope -0.0019; not significant.
cough_grade slope +0.0022; not significant.
appetite_loss_grade slope -0.0009; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
More education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. Overall ANOVA F p=0.69.
Mean PFS: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA p=0.79.
Rural 3.756 vs urban 3.741 (+0.015). Not significant.
smoking_pack_years slope +0.00025; not significant.
education_years slope -0.0025; not significant; direction opposite expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the lung-cancer-style co-mutations / alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss) has a non-zero association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: loop over each genomic flag: stats.ttest_ind(...)
analyses.result_summary: None of the 13 genomic alterations was significantly associated with PFS at alpha=0.05. cdkn2a_loss had the smallest p (-0.042 months, p=0.10). Notably, tp53_mutation (the most prevalent at 45%) had a near-zero effect (+0.003, p=0.87).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each prior therapy flag (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) is individually associated with pfs_months.
More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
More years_since_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: loop over each prior-therapy flag: stats.ttest_ind(...)
analyses.result_summary: None of the five prior-therapy flags was significantly associated with PFS. All |diff|<0.03 months and all p>0.10.
Slope -0.010 months per prior line; not significant.
Slope -0.0007; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 25 SNP carrier flags (snp_rs1045642, snp_rs1065852, ..., snp_rs2070744) shows a significant univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: loop over each snp_*: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ snp', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Across 25 SNPs, only snp_rs4986893 reached nominal p<0.05 (beta -0.092, p=0.014); none survives a Bonferroni threshold of 0.002. snp_rs429358 borderline (+0.047, p=0.064). Consistent with no true germline associations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model including ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, albumin_g_dl, log(1+psa_ng_ml), weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, age_years, hemoglobin_g_dl, bone_mets, and liver_mets, the independent predictors of pfs_months are ecog_ps (negative), albumin (positive), log_PSA (negative), weight_loss (negative), LDH (negative), age (positive), and visceral_mets (small negative). mcrpc, hemoglobin, bone_mets, liver_mets, and alkaline_phosphatase will lose significance after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + albumin_g_dl + log_psa + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + age_years + hemoglobin_g_dl + bone_mets + liver_mets', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Independent significant predictors (all p<0.001): age_years +0.174, ecog_ps -1.150, albumin_g_dl +0.478, log_psa -0.383, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.077, ldh_u_l -0.0005, visceral_mets -0.016. Lost significance after adjustment: mcrpc (p=0.85), alkaline_phosphatase (p=0.27), hemoglobin (p=0.92), bone_mets (p=0.86), liver_mets (p=1.00). Hypothesis confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The brca2_mutation × treatment_olaparib interaction effect on pfs_months remains positive and significant after adjusting for ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin, log_psa, and weight_loss.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps + mcrpc + albumin_g_dl + log_psa + weight_loss_pct_6mo + brca2_mutation*treatment_olaparib', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient +1.62 (p=4e-69). Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA2-mutated patients is robust to adjustment for major prognostic confounders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide differs between mcrpc=1 and mcrpc=0 patients (interaction).
The benefit of treatment_abiraterone differs by mcrpc status (interaction).
The benefit of treatment_docetaxel differs by mcrpc status (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.049, p=0.18. Not significant.
Interaction coef +0.030, p=0.45. Not significant.

Interaction coef +0.080, p=0.042. Nominally significant: docetaxel benefit appears slightly larger in mCRPC patients than non-mCRPC, but small magnitude and would not survive multiple-testing correction across the many interactions tested.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Visceral mets modify the effect of treatment_docetaxel, treatment_enzalutamide, or treatment_abiraterone on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ visceral_mets*tx', df).fit() for each tx
analyses.result_summary: No significant interaction: visceral_mets:treatment_docetaxel coef +0.052 p=0.29; visceral_mets:treatment_enzalutamide coef -0.062 p=0.18; visceral_mets:treatment_abiraterone coef +0.084 p=0.09.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib benefit is larger in patients with pten_loss (HR-deficiency surrogate).
Olaparib benefit is larger in patients with tp53_mutation.
Olaparib benefit is larger in patients with ar_v7_positive.
Olaparib benefit is larger in patients with psma_high.

analyses.result_summary: pten_loss × olaparib interaction +0.299, p=0.025. Nominally significant; magnitude is far smaller than BRCA2 effect (+1.62) and may be a chance finding among many interactions tested.
tp53_mutation × olaparib +0.041, p=0.50. Not significant.
ar_v7_positive × olaparib -0.017, p=0.82. Not significant.
psma_high × olaparib +0.170, p=0.0054. Nominally significant; small magnitude — likely chance finding given many interactions tested.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit is modified by tp53_mutation, prior_immunotherapy, or autoimmune_disease.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ X*treatment_pembrolizumab', df).fit() for X in [tp53_mutation, prior_immunotherapy, autoimmune_disease, msi_high]
analyses.result_summary: No significant interactions. tp53_mutation×pembro -0.041 p=0.63; prior_immunotherapy×pembro -0.022 p=0.86; autoimmune_disease×pembro +0.241 p=0.21; msi_high×pembro -0.340 p=0.15. None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unexpected positive age–PFS association is monotonic across the age distribution and not driven by a few outliers (refining h4).
The age effect on PFS is not modified by treatment assignment (no age × treatment interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS rises monotonically across age quartiles: Q1 (30-58y) 1.56; Q2 (58-65y) 3.13; Q3 (65-72y) 4.33; Q4 (72-90y) 6.00. The positive association is monotonic and large in magnitude.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years*tx', df).fit() for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: All four age×treatment interactions tested were non-significant: age:enzalutamide +0.0012 p=0.21; age:abiraterone -0.0013 p=0.20; age:docetaxel -0.0008 p=0.41; age:olaparib +0.0002 p=0.90. The age effect is consistent across treatment groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG performance status modifies the effect of one or more treatments on pfs_months (treatment×ECOG interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps*tx', df).fit() for each of 6 treatments
analyses.result_summary: All six ecog_ps×treatment interactions non-significant (p=0.29–0.66). No evidence of differential treatment effect by performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive age slope on pfs_months persists in a multivariable model adjusting for ecog_ps, albumin, log_PSA, weight_loss, ldh, and visceral_mets — i.e., it is not confounded by these prognostic covariates.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + log_psa + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + visceral_mets', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coefficient +0.1737 months/year (p≈0). Identical to the unadjusted estimate — the strong positive age association is not confounded by ECOG, albumin, PSA, weight loss, LDH, or visceral disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The brca2_mutation × treatment_olaparib interaction is robust across ecog_ps strata (0, 1, 2) and across mcrpc strata (0, 1).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction is large and significant in every stratum: ECOG 0 +1.69 (p=6e-27); ECOG 1 +1.53 (p=3e-32); ECOG 2 +1.71 (p=7e-16); mCRPC 0 +1.63 (p=1e-28); mCRPC 1 +1.63 (p=6e-36). Effect size is essentially identical across subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The number of concurrent listed treatments (n_tx, sum of the six treatment_* indicators) is associated with pfs_months.

After adjustment for major prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, albumin, log_PSA, weight_loss), the brca2_mutation × treatment_olaparib interaction effect remains the dominant treatment-related signal in the dataset.

analyses.code: df['n_tx']=df[6 tx cols].sum(1); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ n_tx', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: n_tx ranges 0–6 with means: 0→3.76, 1→3.74, 2→3.72, 3→3.80, 4→3.78, 5→3.91, 6→4.07. Slope -0.0001 months per added treatment; p=0.99. No relationship.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + log_psa + weight_loss_pct_6mo + brca2_mutation*treatment_olaparib', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Fully adjusted model: BRCA2:olaparib interaction +1.435 (t=91.1, p≈0). With prognostic covariates entered, BRCA2 main effect (p=0.89) and olaparib main effect (p=0.89) are null — the entire signal is concentrated in the interaction. This is the cleanest predictive-biomarker signature in the dataset.
